title: Child Of Dawn
content:
O gentle vision in the dawn:
    My spirit over faint cool water glides.
    Child of the day,
    To thee;
    And thou art drawn
    By k

title: Unknown Country
content:
Here, in this other world, they come and go
    With easy dream-like movements to and fro.
    They stare through lovely eyes, yet do not seek

title: Milk For The Cat
content:
When the tea is brought at five o'clock,
    And all the neat curtains are drawn with care,
    The little black cat with bright green eyes

title: Thistledown
content:

title: Lake Leman
content:
It is the sacred hour: above the far
    Low emerald hills that northward fold,
    Calmly, upon the blue the evening star
    Floats, wreath

title: Every Thing
content:
Since man has been articulate,

title: Man Carrying Bale
content:
The tough hand closes gently on the load;
     Out of the mind, a voice
    Calls 'Lift!' and the arms, remembering well their work,

title: The Bird At Dawn
content:
What I saw was just one eye
    In the dawn as I was going:
    A bird can carry all the sky
    In that little button glowing.

    Never in

title: Overheard On A Saltmarsh
content:
Nymph, nymph, what are your beads?

    Green glass, goblin. Why do you stare at them?

    Give them me.

                 No.

    Give them

title: Solitude
content:
When you have tidied all things for the night,
    And while your thoughts are fading to their sleep,
    You'll pause a moment in the late fire

title: Children Of Love
content:
The holy boy
    Went from his mother out in the cool of the day
    Over the sun-parched fields
    And in among the olives shining green and

title: Goldfish
content:
They are the angels of that watery world,
    With so much knowledge that they just aspire
    To move themselves on golden fins,

title: The Nightingale Near The House
content:
Here is the soundless cypress on the lawn:
    It listens, listens. Taller trees beyond
    Listen. The moon at the unruffled pond
        Star

title: Week-End
content:
I

    The train! The twelve o'clock for paradise.
     Hurry, or it will try to creep away.
    Out in the country every one is wise:

title: Two Poems
content:

title: Real Property
content:
'Tell me about that harvest field.'
    Oh! Fifty acres of living bread.
    The colour has painted itself in my heart;
    The form is pattern

title: Gravity
content:

    That holds our bodies safely to the earth.

title: Dog
content:
